title: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States reports on the 2020 election of Dr. Todd Fisher as president of the Oklahoma Baptists, weighing what his leadership and that of the new convention officers will mean for the abolition of abortion in Oklahoma. The piece both acknowledges momentum built under former president Blake Gideon and challenges the new leadership to publicly demand passage of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.

questions.question: Who was elected president of the Oklahoma Baptists at the 2020 Annual Meeting and who else was nominated?
questions.answer: Dr. Todd Fisher, pastor of Immanuel Baptist in Shawnee, was elected president; Leron West of Gilcrease Hills Baptist Church in Tulsa and Bill Ascol of Bethel Baptist Church in Owasso were the other nominees.
questions.quote: Dr. Todd Fisher, pastor of Immanuel Baptist in Shawnee, was selected by Oklahoma Baptist messengers at their 2020 Annual Meeting as the next president of the convention. He was nominated for the position by Oklahoma Baptists First Vice President Jeremy Freeman. Leron West of Gilcrease Hills Baptist Church in Tulsa (nominated by former Oklahoma Baptists President Bob Green) and Bill Ascol of Bethel Baptist Church in Owasso (nominated by First Baptist Church of Beggs pastor Chris Gore) were the other nominees.

questions.question: What does Free the States consider to be one of Todd Fisher's primary responsibilities as president of the Oklahoma Baptists?
questions.answer: To demand the passage of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, since that legislation is the only one consistent with the convention's own platform calling for an immediate end to abortion without exception or compromise.
questions.quote: As President, one of his responsibilities is to demand the passage of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, as this is the only legislation consistent with the Oklahoma Baptist platform which states, “[W]e call upon the Oklahoma State Legislature to enact legislation that calls for the immediate end of abortion without exception or compromise.”

questions.question: Why does the article argue that Oklahoma Baptists' stance matters so much to ending abortion in the state?
questions.answer: Because the convention is one of the most influential organizations on social policy in Oklahoma; once its leaders genuinely fight for abolition, abortion will not last long.
questions.quote: Oklahoma Baptists is one of the most influential organizations on social policy in the state. When the convention leaders decide to really fight for the abolition of abortion, abortion’s days will be few.

questions.question: How did former president Blake Gideon move the Oklahoma Baptists toward abolition?
questions.answer: After convention leadership initially opposed SB13 in 2019, Gideon corrected course, preached a sermon supporting anti-abortion immediatism, tweeted that SB13 should receive a committee hearing, and at the 2020 annual meeting publicly called on the state to abolish abortion immediately.
questions.quote: While convention leadership originally came out in opposition to The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13) in 2019, Gideon began correcting course and got the convention moving in the right direction. In 2020, Gideon preached a sermon supporting anti-abortion immediatism and tweeted that SB13 should receive a committee hearing.

questions.question: What did Blake Gideon say at the 2020 annual meeting about abortion?
questions.answer: He affirmed the previous year's resolution against abortion, called abortion murder and a barbarous act against preborn neighbors, called on legislators to end it immediately, and offered the gospel of forgiveness to women who had had abortions.
questions.quote: As President of Oklahoma Baptists, we uphold last year’s resolution against abortion. We believe abortion is murder. It’s a barbarous act against our preborn neighbors. And we still call on state legislators to end abortion immediately. We would like to say to persons who have had abortion that there is forgiveness of sin in Jesus Christ. When we trust Christ as our personal Lord and savior, he separates us from our sin as far as the East is from the West.

questions.question: What criticism did Muskogee Baptist Pastor Brett Baggett level against Oklahoma Baptists' leadership?
questions.answer: Baggett pointed out that the leadership did not vocally support the only 2020 bill that would have ended abortion immediately without exception or compromise.
questions.quote: Did the leadership vocally support the only bill in 2020 that would have ‘ended abortion immediately without exception or compromise’? Sadly, no.

questions.question: What human cost does the article project if the new Oklahoma Baptist leaders fail to push for abolition?
questions.answer: Roughly 10,000 preborn children are likely to be murdered in Oklahoma during the two-year term of the newly elected leaders.
questions.quote: If Fisher and the rest of Oklahoma Baptists’ newly-elected leaders fail to do so, more than 10,000 preborn children are likely to be murdered in Oklahoma during the two-year term that they will serve.

questions.question: How does Free the States ground its political demand on Christian leaders biblically?
questions.answer: By appealing to Isaiah 1:16-17, which calls God's people to cease evil, learn to do good, seek justice, correct oppression, and plead the cause of the defenseless before the civil authorities.
questions.quote: Those who represent the political voice of Oklahoma Baptists (and every Christian denomination) must boldly declare to those in civil authority, “Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean; remove the evil of your deeds from before [God’s] eyes; cease to do evil, learn to do good; seek justice, correct oppression; bring justice to the fatherless, plead the widow’s cause” (Isaiah 1:16-17).

questions.question: Why does the author object to Oklahoma Baptists promoting Rose Day and Abby Johnson?
questions.answer: Rose Day is described as a platform for incrementalist politicians and lobbyists to celebrate "pro-life victories" used as excuses not to abolish abortion, and Abby Johnson is criticized for rejecting both immediate abolition and the fundamentals of Christian salvation by grace alone through faith alone.
questions.quote: Rose Day is an event for the state’s most powerful incrementalist politicians and lobbyists to tout their unjust “pro-life victories” which are used as the excuse not to abolish abortion. As for Johnson, she rejects the convention’s principles on ending abortion immediately and, more importantly, the fundamentals of the Christian faith starting with salvation by grace alone through faith alone.

questions.question: What direct exhortation does Free the States issue to Fisher and the other newly seated Oklahoma Baptist officers?
questions.answer: To bring the Gospel into conflict with the culture of death, demand the total and immediate abolition of abortion without exception or compromise, and refuse to let political leaders settle for anything less than equal protection of law for all image bearers in Oklahoma.
questions.quote: It is our plea and our exhortation that, with all the strength within you, you bring the Gospel into conflict with the culture of death, that you demand the total and immediate abolition of abortion without exception or compromise, and that you do not let our political leaders off the hook with anything less than establishing justice and providing the equal protection of law to all image bearers in Oklahoma.
